title: Riot Platforms Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results and Strategic Highlights
text: Riot Platforms Q1 2026 marks a definitive inflection point as the company officially transitioned into an active, revenue-generating data center operator. Total Revenue: $167.2 million (vs. $161.4 million in Q1 2025). Bitcoin Mining Revenue: $111.9 million (down from $142.9 million). Data Center Revenue: $33.2 million (new revenue stream). Engineering Revenue: $22.2 million (up from $13.9 million). Bitcoin Production: 1,473 BTC mined during the quarter. Cost to Mine: $44,629 per bitcoin (excluding depreciation). Bitcoin Holdings: 15,679 BTC (~$1.1 billion at $68,222/BTC). Cash Position: $282.5 million (including $76.9 million restricted). According to CEO Jason Les: The first quarter of 2026 marks a definitive inflection point for Riot, as we officially transitioned into an active, revenue-generating data center operator. AMD Partnership Expansion: AMD exercised an option for an additional 25 MW of capacity, bringing total contracted data center capacity to 50 MW with the company. Mining revenue declined due to lower bitcoin prices and increased global network competition. Power curtailment credits increased 169% year-over-year, offsetting higher mining costs. Data center operations represent a significant new business direction.
